$comment: FC17 D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR (C23 6.7.1): the constexpr OBJECT storage-class end-to-end. `constexpr int N = 5` dimensions `int data[N]` (sizeof 20 proves N sized the TYPE at translation time); `static constexpr double PI2 = 3.5*2` -> (int)7 (the float-capable fold + static composes); `constexpr int *NULLP = nullptr` == 0 -> 1 (the pointer null-constant arm); block-scope `constexpr char C = 'a'` + `constexpr bool B = true` -> 1+1 (the F2 shared-evaluator char/bool leaf arms). The fill is ARGC-SEEDED so the array CONTENTS are runtime values the release optimizer cannot fold (sum = 10 for argc==1). exit = 20+7+1+10+1+1+2 = 42. Data-model-independent, so the one exit code holds on all four targets incl. the shipped release pipeline. RED-on-revert: without the feature the `constexpr` keyword does not parse.
